summary:
Claim: Rhinomanometry is the gold standard for evaluating nasal obstruction.  
Evidence: - Provides objective, quantitative measurements of airflow resistance. - Differentiates between unilateral/bilateral obstructions and assesses inspiratory/expiratory dynamics.  
Answer: C  

(498 tokens)
